proposed_hypotheses.text: Among 50,000 patients in the AML cohort, the marginal rate of objective_response is approximately 0.19, indicating that the outcome is not rare but is well below 50%.

analyses.result_summary: Overall objective_response rate is 9375/50000 = 0.1875.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_004 (3-level ordinal, likely a performance/severity index) is negatively associated with objective_response: response rate decreases monotonically as feature_004 increases from 0 to 2.
Higher continuous feature_020 (range 0-23) is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher continuous feature_024 (range 20-100) is associated with lower objective_response.
feature_001=1, feature_014=1, and feature_021=1 are each individually associated with higher objective_response than the corresponding =0 group.

analyses.result_summary: Response rates 0.231, 0.174, 0.129 for feature_004 = 0, 1, 2 (chi-square p=1.79e-90).
Mean feature_020: 3.44 in responders vs 3.95 in non-responders (Welch t-test p=1.69e-33).
Mean feature_024: 58.50 in responders vs 60.30 in non-responders (Welch t-test p=2.02e-16).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the binary features showing main effects, feature_026=1 and feature_009=1 are each associated with LOWER objective_response (i.e., they may mark unfavorable disease).

Several plausibly clinical continuous features (feature_022, feature_027, feature_029) are associated with objective_response in directions consistent with prognostic markers (higher feature_022 worse, higher feature_029 better).

analyses.result_summary: feature_022: responders 19.27 vs non 20.39 (p=1.49e-04). feature_027: 5.69 vs 6.17 (p=1.62e-06). feature_029: 3.82 vs 3.80 (p=2.73e-07, positive).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment, the same set of features (feature_004, feature_001, feature_014, feature_021, feature_020, feature_024, feature_022, feature_027, feature_029, feature_026, feature_009) remains independently associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logistic regression (35 features) confirms independent associations of feature_004 (b=-0.36, p=9e-93), feature_014 (b=+0.44, p=2e-71), feature_001 (b=+0.37, p=2e-57), feature_021 (b=+0.36, p=8e-54), feature_020 (b=-0.04, p=3e-31), feature_024 (b=-0.005, p=1e-17), feature_029 (b=+0.12, p=1e-7), feature_026 (b=-0.17, p=6e-7), feature_009 (b=-0.19, p=4e-6), feature_027 (b=-0.006, p=6e-6), feature_022 (b=-0.002, p=1e-4). Pseudo R-squared=0.032.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001, feature_014, and feature_021 are statistically independent of each other (pairwise correlations near zero), suggesting they encode different patient attributes (e.g., a treatment indicator and two distinct biomarkers).

analyses.result_summary: Pairwise Pearson correlations: 001-014 ~0.00, 001-021 ~-0.001, 014-021 ~0.005, all not significant. Joint distribution close to product of marginals (~40% x 30% x 40% = 4.8% triple-positive; observed 2401/50000 = 4.8%).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There are pairwise (two-way) interactions among feature_001, feature_014, feature_021 such that joint positivity confers additional benefit beyond their main effects.

analyses.result_summary: All three two-way interaction coefficients are non-significant on their own (p=0.46, 0.085, 0.50). Pairwise interactions alone are too weak to explain the joint pattern. Suggests a higher-order (3-way) effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a strong three-way interaction such that response is dramatically elevated only when feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 simultaneously, with response rate near baseline (~16-17%) in every other 3-way cell.

analyses.result_summary: Cell rates: (0,0,0)=0.172; (0,0,1)=0.162; (0,1,0)=0.160; (0,1,1)=0.157; (1,0,0)=0.164; (1,0,1)=0.169; (1,1,0)=0.160; (1,1,1)=0.633 (n=2401). Three-way interaction coef in logit = +2.13 (OR ~8.4), p=7e-92. All main effects and 2-way interactions are non-significant after including the triple term.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-positive subgroup (feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1), there is further heterogeneity: some patients show ~80% response while others show ~16%, suggesting one or more resistance markers suppress benefit.

analyses.result_summary: Treatment-by-feature interaction screen for feature_021 (treating it as the candidate treatment) flags feature_014 (p=5e-102) and feature_001 (p=1e-78) as the dominant positive modifiers, and feature_009 (p=5e-7, coef=-0.41) and feature_026 (p=6e-6, coef=-0.31) as significant NEGATIVE modifiers — i.e., feature_009=1 or feature_026=1 reduce the treatment-associated benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-positive subgroup (feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1), feature_009=1 reduces objective_response from ~70% to ~19%.
Within the triple-positive subgroup, feature_026=1 reduces objective_response from ~72% to ~18%.

analyses.result_summary: In triple-positive (n=2401): feature_009=0 rate=0.691 (n=2125); feature_009=1 rate=0.188 (n=276). Difference = -0.503.
In triple-positive (n=2401): feature_026=0 rate=0.717 (n=2025); feature_026=1 rate=0.184 (n=376). Difference = -0.533.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_009 and feature_026 act as independent suppressors: even when both are in their unfavorable state, response is at baseline (~19%); when both are favorable (=0) the response rate is ~78.6%.

analyses.result_summary: In triple-positive cohort: (009=0, 026=0): 0.786 (n=1790); (009=0, 026=1): 0.182 (n=335); (009=1, 026=0): 0.187 (n=235); (009=1, 026=1): 0.195 (n=41). Either resistance marker abolishes the benefit; neither dominates.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The full 5-feature subgroup (feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_009=0 AND feature_026=0; ~3.6% of cohort) has a markedly elevated objective_response rate (~78.6%) compared to all other patients (~16.5%).

analyses.result_summary: Subgroup size 1790 (3.58%); subgroup response 0.7860, complement response 0.1653 (diff=+0.621). Logit: fullsub coef=+2.92 (OR ~18.5), p<1e-300, z=49.6.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The 5-feature subgroup effect on objective_response remains highly significant after adjustment for prognostic continuous covariates (feature_004, feature_020, feature_024, feature_022, feature_027, feature_029).

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ fullsub + feature_004 + feature_020 + feature_024 + feature_022 + feature_027 + feature_029)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted logit fullsub coef=+3.00 (OR ~20), p<1e-300; effect actually slightly larger after adjustment because the subgroup tends to have somewhat worse prognostic features. All prognostic covariates retain their main effects (feature_004 b=-0.39, feature_020 b=-0.04, feature_024 b=-0.006, feature_029 b=+0.13).

proposed_hypotheses.text: If feature_021 is the treatment indicator, then the treatment effect of feature_021 is approximately zero in the cohort overall and entirely concentrated in the biomarker-eligible subgroup defined by feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_009=0 AND feature_026=0.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ tx + bio + tx:bio) with tx=feature_021, bio=biomarker_pos
analyses.result_summary: tx main effect coef=-0.02 (p=0.42); bio main effect coef=-0.06 (p=0.25); tx-by-bio interaction coef=+2.99 (p<1e-200, z=36.5). Treatment effect outside subgroup: rate(tx=1)=0.164 vs rate(tx=0)=0.167 (diff=-0.003). Treatment effect inside subgroup: rate(tx=1)=0.786 vs rate(tx=0)=0.158 (diff=+0.628). The treatment is essentially inert except in the biomarker-defined subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The structure is symmetric in feature_001, feature_014, feature_021 — i.e., interaction-screen results would also flag the other two as the dominant modifiers if either were treated as 'the treatment', so the data alone cannot disambiguate which is the drug indicator vs. biomarker.

analyses.result_summary: With feature_014 as the candidate treatment, top interactions are feature_001 (p=7e-103), feature_021 (p=5e-102), feature_026 (p=1e-16), feature_009 (p=7e-8). With feature_001 as candidate treatment, top interactions are feature_014 (p=7e-103), feature_021 (p=1e-78), feature_026 (p=3e-8), feature_009 (p=5e-4). Effect structure is symmetric: any of the three could be labeled the treatment with the other two as biomarkers, but the same 5-feature subgroup defines responders.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the biomarker-eligible subgroup, the treatment effect of feature_021 (or feature_001 or feature_014) is reduced in higher feature_004 levels (more advanced disease), but remains very large at every level (>+0.55 absolute).

analyses.result_summary: Within eligible (n=4531): feature_004=0 -> tx=1: 0.859, tx=0: 0.194, diff=+0.665 (n=1542); feature_004=1 -> 0.772 vs 0.153, diff=+0.619 (n=2310); feature_004=2 -> 0.668 vs 0.098, diff=+0.570 (n=679). Treatment effect attenuates modestly with higher feature_004 but remains very large.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous prognostic features (feature_020, feature_022, feature_024, feature_027, feature_029) do NOT meaningfully modify the treatment effect within the biomarker-eligible subgroup; they exert prognostic effects on outcome but do not change which patients benefit from treatment.

analyses.code: stratified analysis of treatment effect by feature_020 quantile within eligible subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Within eligible (n=4531): low-feature_020 half -> tx=1: 0.809, tx=0: 0.173, diff=+0.636 (n=2280); high-feature_020 half -> 0.764 vs 0.143, diff=+0.621 (n=2251). Difference in treatment effect between halves is small (~+0.015), supporting feature_020 as a prognostic but not predictive marker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: No continuous feature (any of the 13 continuous features in the dataset) shows a treatment-by-feature interaction with feature_021 that survives multiple-comparison correction beyond the binary modifiers already identified.

analyses.result_summary: Continuous-feature interactions with feature_021 ranked by p: feature_024 p=3.5e-3 (coef=+0.07 standardized); feature_020 p=0.039; feature_035 p=0.047; feature_022 p=0.083. None survive Bonferroni correction (alpha=0.05/13 ~=0.0038), and none come close to the binary modifiers' p-values (~1e-100).

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive 4-binary-feature subgroup search confirms that every high-response (>50%) subgroup contains feature_001=1, feature_014=1, feature_021=1 as its 'core', with the highest rate further requiring feature_026=0 or feature_009=0.

analyses.code: exhaustive enumeration of all 4-binary combos with all 16 polarities, n>=200
analyses.result_summary: Top 4-binary subgroups (rate > 0.5): (001=1,026=0,014=1,021=1) rate=0.717 (n=2025); (001=1,014=1,009=0,021=1) rate=0.691 (n=2125); rest of top 15 are similar combinations with one extra binary held fixed. Every high-response subgroup contains the (001=1,014=1,021=1) core.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The relationship between count of 'favorable' values among (feature_001=1, feature_014=1, feature_021=1, feature_009=0, feature_026=0) and objective_response is non-additive: rates are flat (~16-17%) at 0,1,2,3, or 4 favorable values, then jump to ~78% only at 5/5 — a pure AND-gate structure.
The 5-feature subgroup signal is reproducible: it shows a similar effect size in random split-halves of the cohort, ruling out single-cell artifact or overfitting.

analyses.code: random 25k/25k split; compute fullsub vs. complement response in each
analyses.result_summary: Half 1 (n=25000): subgroup rate 0.771 vs complement 0.167 (diff=+0.604, n_sub=898). Half 2 (n=25000): subgroup rate 0.802 vs complement 0.164 (diff=+0.638, n_sub=892). Effect is highly consistent.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Outside the biomarker-eligible subgroup, the feature_021 'treatment' indicator has zero net effect on objective_response (i.e., the drug provides no benefit to biomarker-negative patients).

analyses.result_summary: In biomarker-ineligible subset (n=45469): rate(feature_021=1)=0.1640 vs rate(feature_021=0)=0.1669 (diff=-0.003, p=0.42). No clinically or statistically meaningful treatment effect outside the molecular subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among prognostic features, feature_004 (3-level severity) has the largest independent effect on outcome (each unit decreasing log-odds by ~0.39); feature_020, feature_024, and feature_022 have smaller continuous effects in the same prognostic direction.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ fullsub + feature_004 + feature_020 + feature_024 + feature_022 + feature_027 + feature_029)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-odds coefficients (negative = unfavorable for response): feature_004 -0.39 (p<1e-100); feature_020 -0.04 (p=2e-34); feature_024 -0.006 (p=1e-18); feature_022 -0.002 (p=2e-4); feature_027 -0.007 (p=4e-6); feature_029 +0.13 (p=8e-8). All independent of subgroup membership.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS: assuming feature_021 is the treatment indicator, the treatment substantially increases objective_response (rate(treated)=0.786 vs rate(untreated)=0.158; absolute increase ~+0.63) ONLY in patients with feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_009=0 AND feature_026=0; no detectable treatment effect outside this subgroup.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ tx + bio + tx:bio + feature_004 + feature_020 + feature_024 + feature_029) with tx=feature_021 and bio=biomarker_pos
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted treatment-by-subgroup interaction coef=+3.06 (OR=21.3), p<1e-200; tx main effect coef=-0.02 (p=0.54); biomarker main effect coef=-0.06 (p=0.32). All prognostic covariates retain effects. Treatment effect is essentially zero in the 91% biomarker-negative population and very large in the 9% biomarker-positive population.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The same predictive subgroup (feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_009=0 AND feature_026=0) defines treatment benefit regardless of which of feature_001, feature_014, or feature_021 is interpreted as the treatment indicator; i.e., the subgroup is a property of the data, not the labeling.
Among lower-prognostic-risk (feature_004=0) patients in the biomarker-eligible subgroup who receive treatment (feature_021=1), the response rate exceeds 85%, the highest in the cohort.

analyses.code: symmetric stratified analyses with each binary as the candidate treatment
analyses.result_summary: Treating feature_001 as treatment yields the same +0.63 absolute response rate increase in biomarker-positive (014=1, 021=1, 009=0, 026=0) subset; treating feature_014 as treatment yields the same effect in (001=1, 021=1, 009=0, 026=0). The five-feature subgroup is invariant to which binary is labeled the 'drug' indicator.

feature_004=0 within eligible+treated: response rate 0.859 (n=617); feature_004=1: 0.772 (n=902); feature_004=2: 0.668 (n=271). The most favorable cell (low feature_004 + biomarker-eligible + treated) reaches 86%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Summary: this AML cohort is best described by a single five-feature predictive subgroup that confers a ~63 percentage-point increase in objective_response, plus a small set of independent prognostic main effects (feature_004, feature_020, feature_024, feature_022, feature_027, feature_029). No additional important higher-order interactions were detected.

analyses.code: final combined logit model + sensitivity check
analyses.result_summary: Final picture: (1) the 5-feature subgroup (feature_001=1 & feature_014=1 & feature_021=1 & feature_009=0 & feature_026=0) accounts for the bulk of explainable variance in objective_response (subgroup OR ~20 adjusted); (2) feature_004 is the strongest independent prognostic factor (per-level OR ~0.68); (3) feature_020, feature_024, feature_022, feature_027, feature_029 contribute smaller but robust prognostic effects; (4) sex-like (feature_010), feature_005, feature_011 (likely age) and most other binaries show no association. The data are consistent with a precision-medicine paradigm: a targeted therapy that requires two co-positive biomarkers and the absence of two resistance markers in order to produce response.
